context: Tests whether the agent uses the cron package for sale scheduling with the correct cron expression, includes a real-time sale lookup as a fallback, implements the queue using a Redis Sorted Set with correct operations, sets admission token TTL to 300 seconds, returns 1-indexed queue position, and includes bot-protection measures.

checklist.name: CronJob import
checklist.description: Uses CronJob from the 'cron' package (import { CronJob } from 'cron') rather than node-cron, setInterval, or another scheduling library

checklist.name: Per-minute cron expression
checklist.description: The cron job uses the expression '* * * * *' (every minute) for sale status transitions

checklist.name: Activate scheduled sales
checklist.description: The cron job includes a query to transition sales from 'scheduled' to 'active' where starts_at <= NOW() and ends_at > NOW()

checklist.name: End expired sales
checklist.description: The cron job includes a query to transition sales from 'active' to 'ended' where ends_at <= NOW()

checklist.name: Real-time sale lookup
checklist.description: A function (e.g. getActiveSale) performs a live DB query with starts_at <= now AND ends_at > now checks, not relying solely on the status field set by the cron job

checklist.name: Redis Sorted Set for queue
checklist.description: The queue is backed by a Redis Sorted Set (zadd) where the score is the join timestamp (Date.now() or equivalent epoch value)

checklist.name: zpopmin for batch admission
checklist.description: Batch admission uses redis.zpopmin() (or equivalent atomic sorted set pop) rather than zrange + zrem

checklist.name: 1-indexed queue position
checklist.description: The queue join function returns the position as (zrank result + 1), i.e., 1-indexed, not 0-indexed

checklist.name: Admission token TTL 300s
checklist.description: Admission tokens are stored with a TTL of exactly 300 seconds (5 minutes) using redis.setex() or equivalent

checklist.name: Token admission check
checklist.description: The isAdmitted function checks the Redis admission token key and returns true only when the token value equals '1'

checklist.name: Bot protection measures
checklist.description: DESIGN.md or code comments mention at least two of the following: rate limiting on queue join, CAPTCHA requirement, IP velocity checks

checklist.name: Token expiry re-queue rationale
checklist.description: DESIGN.md explains that expired admission tokens cause the slot to be released or returned to the queue (customers who don't complete checkout lose their spot)
